calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
Off Calendar Sep-06-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Worth in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that this is a petition to confirm trust assets going to 2 separate groups of assets, that the issue regarding both groups is that the trust is dealing with the indication that Ms. Ponzecchi has life interests but on her death it goes into the estate and that the question is if she is entitled to redirect post death interests. Mr. Worth stated that as to the 6 trusts the only asset is accrued income to be allocated as a trust asset. The Court stated that there is no general assignment, and that it talks about title but also talks about assignment. Mr. Worth gave his interpretation of Ms. Ponzecchi's intent. The Court stated that this specifically transfers interests under a certificate, that it talks about intent but does not state "I hereby assign". Mr. Worth stated that in regards to the monies she was receiving from the trustee she said numerous times that the monies were to go into the trust account. The Court directed Mr. Worth back to the 1949 trust. Mr. Worth stated that the administrator would be the executor of her will and that it would go through her trust. The Court asked if it was an amendment of her trust to do whatever her estate does. Mr. Worth stated that her estate does pour over into her trust. The Court stated that the existence of a pour over will does not get you there, that Heggstad is usually titled in an individuals name with indication that it was meant to go into her trust. Mr. Worth stated that the trust itself is not amendable which is why she gave the assignment, that her intent and feeling was that it was her asset. The Court stated that that is not enough to amend. Mr. Worth stated that if the Court is ruling against the 1949 trust they will have to probate in Los Angeles and confirmed that the asset is $700,000.00. The Court stated that it may not have been the greatest estate plan but that is how it was written and denied the Petition for Order Confirming Trust assets.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)
